title: Purification of FIP200 CTR-GFP

steps: To purify GFP-FIP200(CTR), as described previously (Turco et al. 2019 Mol Cell, PMID: 30853400), fuse the C-terminal domain of FIP200 (1458-1594aa) to a N-terminal 6xHis-TEV-GFP-tag through cloning into a pET-DUET1 vector (available on Addgene).

After the transformation of the pET-DUET1 vector encoding 6xHis-TEV-GFP-FIP200(CTR) in E. coli Rosetta pLysS cells (Novagen Cat# 70956-4), grow cells in 2x Tryptone Yeast extract (TY) medium at 37 °C until an OD 600 of 0.4 and then continued at 18 °C .
Once the cells reached an OD 600 of 0.8, induce the protein expression with 100 micromolar (µM) isopropyl β-D-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG) for 16:00:00 at 18 °C .
Collect the cells by centrifugation and resuspend in lysis buffer, complete EDTA-free protease inhibitors (Roche), CIP protease inhibitor (Sigma), and DNase (Sigma)). Lysis buffer: %s %s
Clear lysates by centrifugation at 18000 rpm, 4 °C, 45 minutes in a SORVAL RC6+ centrifuge with an F21S-8x50Y rotor (Thermo Scientific).
Filter the supernatant through an 0.45 µm filter and loaded onto a pre-equilibrated 5 ml His-Trap HP column (Cytiva).
After binding of His tagged proteins to the column, wash the column with three column volumes of wash buffer. Wash buffer: %s %s
Elute the proteins with a stepwise imidazole gradient (30, 75, 100, 150, 225, 300 mM).
Pool and incuabte the fractions containing the 6xHis-TEV-GFP-FIP200(CTR) Overnight with TEV protease at 4 °C .
After cleave off the 6xHis tag , recapture 6xHis tag and His-tagged TEV protease with nickel beads for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .

Pellet the beads by centrifugation and the supernatant, concentrate containing the GFP-FIP200(CTR) protein using a 30 kDa cut-off Amicon filter (Merck Millipore) and load onto a pre-equilibrated Superdex 200 Increase 10/300 GL column (Cytiva).
Elute the proteins with SEC buffer. SEC buffer: %s %s
Analyse the fractions by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining.
After concentrating the purified protein, aliquot the protein and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen.
creator: Elias Adriaenssens
